sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI ITRA No. 38 and 39 of 2023 Date Order with Signature of Judge Hearing of Case (Priority) 1. For order on office objection No. 24 2. For hearing of Main Case 20.01.2026 Mr. Qaim Ali Memon, Advocate for the Applicant Mr. Muhammad Nawab Awan, Advocate for the Respondent Pursuant to Order dated 20.10.2025, learned counsel for the Respondent states upon instructions that it may be in the interest of justice and revenue to set aside the impugned order and remand the matter back to the learned tribunal for adjudication afresh in accordance with law expeditiously, preferably within 90 days. Learned counsel further seeks that pending the aforesaid, no coercive action be taken against the respondent arising here from. Learned counsel for the applicant articulates no cavil in such regard and states that both reference applications may be disposed of in the said terms. Order accordingly. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of the Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Office to place copy of this order in the captioned reference applications. Judge Judge Amjad PS
